spec_match.rationale: The page benchmarks `Map.prototype.upsert(key, updateFn, insertFn)` — that signature is an obsolete revision of the proposal. The version that actually shipped in Chrome 143 (per chromestatus 5201653661827072 and stage-4 tc39/proposal-upsert) is `getOrInsert(key, default)` and `getOrInsertComputed(key, factory)`. The whole demo therefore measures a method the browser does not implement and would only ever run via its own polyfill, so the 'native upsert used' branch is dead code in conformant Chrome 143.

interactive.rationale: Feature-detect dot, benchmark runner, canvas bar chart, code-tabs, and three preset 'Run' buttons each invoke real code and update DOM state. Genuinely interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb promises a `Map.prototype.upsert` benchmark with feature detect + polyfill + three presets; the page delivers, but the blurb itself is wrong about the shipped method name, so blurb and page agree only because they make the same mistake.

edge_cases.rationale: Polyfill fallback is wired and a winner badge is set; but the timings will always favour the JS implementations equally because feature detection of the wrong name causes the polyfill to fire universally — there is no edge case where users see the native vs polyfill timing contrast the page promises.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link present on the parent feature index (referenced from the crumb). Adding the chromestatus link directly into this concept page's see-also would also be nice for self-heal.

design_system.rationale: Variables used for most surfaces, but the bar-chart fillStyle uses raw hex values (`#000000`, `#0022ff`, `#0a8f5c`, `#f4f3ee`, `#666`, `#444`) and the chart background `#f4f3ee` is not one of the documented design-system tokens. Should pull from CSS variables via `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--accent-blue')` etc.

openQuestions.title: Rebuild benchmark around getOrInsert / getOrInsertComputed
openQuestions.detail: Replace the `Map.prototype.upsert(updateFn, insertFn)` pattern with the shipped API: pattern 3 should call `map.set(w, map.getOrInsert(w, 0) + 1)` for the counter use case, and `map.getOrInsertComputed(k, () => …)` for the nested/grouping cases. The feature detect should probe `Map.prototype.getOrInsert` and `Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed`, not `Map.prototype.upsert`. Until this lands the page is teaching the wrong method.

openQuestions.title: Add the direct chromestatus.com link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Other v143 concept pages include `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5201653661827072` directly in the concept's see-also list (this is also the self-heal anchor for the slug-recovery tooling). This page only links upward to the feature index; add the direct chromestatus link here as well.

openQuestions.title: Pull chart colours from CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: `drawBarChart` hard-codes `#000`, `#0022ff`, `#0a8f5c`, `#f4f3ee`, `#666`, `#444`. Replace with values read from `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement)` against the documented `--text-black`, `--accent-blue`, `--accent-emerald`, `--bg-stone`, `--text-muted`, `--text-charcoal` tokens so the canvas tracks design-system changes.

openQuestions.title: Show variance, not just median
openQuestions.detail: 5 runs × 50 000 words and only the median is shown. A min/median/max strip would let visitors see how stable each pattern is and would harden the 'fastest' claim against a single lucky run.

summary: Visually polished benchmark, but the spec-match problem is fundamental: it's measuring `Map.prototype.upsert(updateFn, insertFn)`, which is an older revision of the proposal that never shipped. Chrome 143 ships `getOrInsert` / `getOrInsertComputed`. Rewriting the third pattern (and the feature-detect) around the actual shipped methods is a major correction. Secondary fixes: hex colours into variables and a direct chromestatus link.
